refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use by minimizing data acquisition latency and processing latency.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the overall latency requirement, the update process is decomposed into data acquisition and data processing steps, each contributing to total latency. This allows targeted design parameters to address each latency source.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure and verify the p95 latency of data acquisition and data processing separately, then combined, ensuring total update latency meets ≤ 1.0 s or customer target.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process and update worksite model with minimal latency

candidate_dps: DP1: Use high-bandwidth, low-latency telemetry for data acquisition
DP2: Implement real-time data processing algorithms optimized for low latency
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1 and DP2 combined to address acquisition and processing latency

potential_sources: Data acquisition latency affects processing start time
Processing algorithm efficiency depends on data format from acquisition
coupling_check.notes: The two DPs are logically coupled but can be designed with modular interfaces to minimize tight coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes telemetry network can support required bandwidth and latency
Risk that processing algorithms may not meet latency targets under peak load
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor telemetry network performance and processing algorithm efficiency during integration to mitigate risks.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for telemetry data acquisition and real-time processing to update the worksite model

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives raw sensor and vehicle data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated worksite model and commands to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication between NGHE Solution and external modules for data exchange

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes update logic and data processing, minimizing diffusion and maximizing containment.
Primarily data source; embedding update logic here would increase diffusion.
Focuses on operator control; not suitable for update processing.
Safety monitoring only; unrelated to update latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Placing update latency logic within NGHE Solution module centralizes responsibility and minimizes cross-module diffusion, improving maintainability and performance.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module becomes overloaded
Dependency on telemetry network performance external to NGHE Solution
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider load balancing and modular subcomponents within NGHE Solution to mitigate bottlenecks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement clearly decomposes latency requirement into acquisition and processing sub-FRs; selected DPs logically address these with manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as update logic is centralized in NGHE Solution module
Interfaces are well-defined and limited to known interfaces
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor NGHE Solution as the best architectural boundary for update latency logic.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring latency end-to-end and at interfaces using telemetry and tasking data flows.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of update latency responsibility to NGHE Solution module with defined interfaces to Vehicle and Teleoperation.

recommended_revisions: Monitor telemetry network performance as a critical external factor
Plan for scalability within NGHE Solution to handle peak data loads

decision.justification: Current level of decomposition and assignment meets requirements with clear FR->DP->IM mapping, manageable coupling, and well-defined interfaces. No immediate need to zigzag or revise.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process and update worksite model with minimal latency

revise.why: No deficiencies identified requiring revision at this level.

assumptions: Telemetry network supports required bandwidth and latency
Processing algorithms can be optimized for real-time performance
open_risks: Processing load spikes causing latency breaches
evidence_needed: Latency measurements from telemetry and processing modules under operational conditions
Load testing results for NGHE Solution processing capabilities
